name: v2 tree-schema annotations on truncated 1500-char problems (haiku judge verdicts)
short_description: Per-row v2 hierarchical decompositions produced by claude-haiku-4-5 with 1500-char-truncated problem text in BOTH annotator and judge prompts; matched to the t0014 sonnet 20-row pool to decompose the v2-vs-v1 acceptance gap into a pure schema effect and a pure text-length effect.
model_description: Annotator and judge are both claude-haiku-4-5 (Anthropic) accessed via the local Claude Code CLI. The annotator emits the v2 tree schema (global, subtasks[], global_atomics, gold_actions). The judge issues a binary acceptable / needs revision verdict. Both prompts truncate the problem text to 1500 chars with an explicit 'Problem (truncated to 1500 chars):' header.
prediction_schema: Each line is a JSON object with fields: _pilot_row_index (int), task_id (string), benchmark (string), domain (string), difficulty (string|null), problem (string), problem_excerpt_limit (int = 1500), hierarchy (object: global, subtasks[], global_atomics), gold_actions (object), annotation_model (string), annotator_notes (string), hierarchy_completeness (boolean), judge_verdict (string: 'acceptable' or 'needs revision'), judge_justification (string), judge_model (string).

files.description: Per-row v2 hierarchical decompositions with truncated-context judge verdicts on the 20-row matched pool from t0014.
